title: Tier 5 — 1ère (Lycée)
prompt:
You are taking a TIER 5 technical evaluation for STANDARD models.

Solve 5 JavaScript exercises inspired by a high school curriculum (1ère).

Return your answers in Markdown using headers for the exercise IDs and code blocks for the JavaScript functions.

[EXERCISE Math]
Write a JavaScript function "moyennePonderee(notes)" that takes an array of {valeur, coeff} objects and returns the weighted average. Return 0 if array is empty.

[EXERCISE Francais]
Write a JavaScript function "frequenceMots(phrase)" that returns an object with the count of each word in the string. Assume space separation.

[EXERCISE SVT]
Write a JavaScript function "transcrireADN(adn)" that converts 'A'->'U', 'T'->'A', 'C'->'G', 'G'->'C' (ADN to ARN).

[EXERCISE Histoire]
Write a JavaScript function "grouperParEpoque(evenements)" that takes an array of {nom, epoque} and returns an object grouping names by 'epoque'.

[EXERCISE Info]
Write a JavaScript class "FeuTricolore" with a method "passerAuSuivant()" that cycles a 'couleur' property through 'rouge' -> 'vert' -> 'orange' -> 'rouge'. Constructor starts at 'rouge'.

Expected strict response format:
{
  "math": "function moyennePonderee(...) { ... }",
  "francais": "function frequenceMots(...) { ... }",
  "svt": "function transcrireADN(...) { ... }",
  "histoire": "function grouperParEpoque(...) { ... }",
  "info": "class FeuTricolore { ... }"
}

[ALGORITHMIC EXERCISES — code pur, sans culture générale]
Return each function using a Markdown header (### <exercise id>) followed by a ```javascript code block.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "convertirBase(n, base)" that converts a non-negative integer n to its string representation in the given base (2-16). Use lowercase letters for digits above 9.

[EXERCISE algo_facile_2]
Write a JavaScript function "kemePlusGrand(tab, k)" that returns the k-th largest distinct value (1-indexed). Assume valid k.

[EXERCISE algo_moyen_1]
Write a JavaScript function "compteurFrequence(tab)" that returns an object mapping each element to its count in the array.

[EXERCISE algo_difficile_1]
Write a JavaScript function "exponentiationRapide(x, n)" that returns x raised to n (n >= 0) using fast exponentiation (exponentiation by squaring).

[EXERCISE algo_defi]
Write a JavaScript function "sousTableauMax(tab)" that returns the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray (Kadane algorithm).

evaluations.call: (()=>{ const f = new FeuTricolore(); f.passerAuSuivant(); return f.couleur; })()

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Conversion de base
tasks.hint: n.toString(base) renvoie la représentation en base donnée (lettres minuscules au-delà de 9).

tasks.label: Algorithmique — K-ième plus grand
tasks.hint: Valeurs distinctes triées décroissant, indice k-1.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Fréquence des éléments
tasks.hint: Boucle for...of avec un objet accumulant les comptes : obj[v] = (obj[v] || 0) + 1.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Exponentiation rapide
tasks.hint: Si n pair : (x*x)^(n/2) ; si n impair : x * x^(n-1) ; cas n=0 -> 1.

tasks.label: Algorithmique — Sous-tableau de somme max
tasks.hint: Algorithme de Kadane : max global et max se terminant à l'indice courant.
